inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** тАФ Lost guard against copying outDir into itself тАФ The old `copyDir` in `utils.ts` had an explicit guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) to skip copying the output directory into itself when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g., `publicDir: '.'` or `outDir: 'public/build'`). The replacement `fs.cpSync` call has no equivalent protection. This is a behavioral regression for nested-output configurations. A `filter` predicate should be added to skip the resolved `outDir`.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** тАФ Symlink semantics flipped from dereference to preserve тАФ The old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` and `fs.copyFileSync` which dereference symlinks, flattening them to real files/dirs. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, preserving symlinks verbatim. A relative symlink or one pointing outside the copied tree may now appear broken in the output. If old semantics are desired, pass `dereference: true`.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** тАФ Symlink semantics flipped from dereference to preserve тАФ Same symlink semantics change as in `prepareOutDir.ts`. The old hand-rolled copy dereferenced symlinks; `fs.cpSync` without `dereference: true` preserves them. A symlink in the template directory may end up broken in the scaffolded project.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

The stdlib migration from hand-rolled `copyDir` to `fs.cpSync` is the right direction, but introduces two behavioral regressions: a lost guard against copying `outDir` into itself for nested-output configurations, and symlink semantics silently flipping from dereference to preserve. Both should be addressed before merge.

**3** finding(s): ЁЯФ┤ 0  ┬╖  ЁЯЯб 1  ┬╖  ЁЯФ╡ 2

- ЁЯЯб `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ Lost guard against copying outDir into itself тАФ The old `copyDir` in `utils.ts` had an explicit guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) to skip copying the output directory into itself when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g., `publicDir: '.'` or `outDir: 'public/build'`). The replacement `fs.cpSync` call has no equivalent protection. This is a behavioral regression for nested-output configurations. A `filter` predicate should be added to skip the resolved `outDir`.

  ```suggestion
  fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, filter: (src) => path.resolve(src) !== path.resolve(outDir), mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE, })
  ```
- ЁЯФ╡ `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ Symlink semantics flipped from dereference to preserve тАФ The old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` and `fs.copyFileSync` which dereference symlinks, flattening them to real files/dirs. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, preserving symlinks verbatim. A relative symlink or one pointing outside the copied tree may now appear broken in the output. If old semantics are desired, pass `dereference: true`.
- ЁЯФ╡ `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ Symlink semantics flipped from dereference to preserve тАФ Same symlink semantics change as in `prepareOutDir.ts`. The old hand-rolled copy dereferenced symlinks; `fs.cpSync` without `dereference: true` preserves them. A symlink in the template directory may end up broken in the scaffolded project.
